title: Tamil Kozhi Rasam (Chicken Rasam)

ingredients.name: chicken with bone (neck, back or small pieces)

subs.name: skip chicken for a vegetarian pepper rasam; the rest stays the same
1.5 tbsp tamarind paste diluted in 80 ml water
1 tsp coriander powder + 0.5 tsp cumin + 0.5 tsp pepper — mix and use

steps.text: Soak tamarind in 100 ml warm water for 10 minutes; squeeze and strain to extract the juice. In a small saucepan, combine chicken pieces, 700 ml water, turmeric and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil and skim any foam.

Reduce to a steady simmer and cook the chicken for 15 minutes until the meat is cooked through and the broth is lightly flavoured. Lift out the chicken pieces and shred any meat from the bones; discard the bones. Reserve the stock.
In a kadai or heavy pot, heat oil on medium. Splutter mustard seeds and cumin, then add dried red chillies, curry leaves, garlic and asafoetida. Fry for 30 seconds.

Add the tomatoes and crush them against the pan. Add rasam powder, crushed pepper and the tamarind extract. Pour in the reserved chicken stock and bring to a rolling boil. Cook for 5 minutes until the raw tamarind smell mellows.
Add the shredded chicken meat and any remaining broth. Simmer 2 minutes, taste and correct salt. The rasam should be thin and peppery. Turn off the heat.
Finish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve as a first course poured over rice, or sip it from a tumbler — it is a well-known remedy for sore throats and head colds across Tamil households.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rasam-powder → 1 tsp coriander powder + 0.5 tsp cumin + 0.5 tsp pepper — mix and use; tamarind → 1.5 tbsp tamarind paste diluted in 80 ml water; chicken → skip chicken for a vegetarian pepper rasam; the rest stays the same; dried-red-chilli → 0.5 tsp extra rasam powder
